commands.review_summary:
The PR narrows the session tool-result guard’s synthetic flush boundary, updates transcript repair to move late real tool results before synthesizing placeholders, and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +44, Tests +71. Total +115 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: no. live high-confidence reproduction was run. Source inspection and the PR's focused tests show the current guard can synthesize at the new-assistant-tool-call boundary and the patch exercises that ordering shape.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review should require real after-fix runtime/session proof, clean CI, and an explicit decision on whether the linked lock-leak field report is separate scope; there is no narrow code defect for ClawSweeper to repair.

target.title: fix(agents): avoid synthetic tool results during parallel races (#88168)
target.body:
## Summary

- Stops the session tool-result guard from inserting synthetic missing-result entries at the moment a newer assistant tool-call turn arrives while older tool results may still be in flight.
- Lets transcript repair move late real tool results back next to their matching assistant tool calls before synthesizing placeholders.
- Preserves missing-result repair for genuinely absent tool results and duplicate/orphan cleanup.
- Intentionally out of scope: changing Anthropic streaming/tool execution ordering outside the session transcript guard and repair layer.

## Linked context

Closes #88168

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Parallel tool-call batches should not persist `[openclaw] missing tool result...` placeholders before real in-flight tool results have a chance to land and be repaired into provider-valid order.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout at `/private/tmp/openclaw-88168` on macOS, branch `fix/parallel-tool-result-race-88168`, running the repo's real Vitest and tsgo test runners against the changed code.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `timeout 180 node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts src/agents/session-transcript-repair.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): The focused test run passed 1 Vitest shard with `2 passed / 73 tests`. `git diff --check` exited 0. Type checking exited 0 for `timeout 240 node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src-88168.tsbuildinfo`.
- Observed result after fix: A new regression test persists two assistant tool-call turns followed by a real result for the first call and verifies no synthetic missing-result text is written; transcript repair moves late real results into strict assistant/result order without adding synthetic entries.
- What was not tested: A live Anthropic Claude session with real parallel `exec`/`read` calls was not run from this workstation.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This is deterministic source-repro proof using repository tests rather than live provider proof; the original production session JSONL and Anthropic credentials are not available here.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Issue #88168 documents live sessions where real parallel tool side effects completed but persisted transcript history contained synthetic `[openclaw] missing tool result in session history; inserted synthetic error result for transcript repair.` entries instead of the real outputs.

## Tests and validation

- `timeout 180 node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts src/agents/session-transcript-repair.test.ts`
- `timeout 240 node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src-88168.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check`

Regression coverage was added for the guard not synthesizing at the new-assistant boundary and for transcript repair moving late real results ahead of newer assistant tool calls.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Parallel tool-call transcripts should retain real results instead of premature synthetic error placeholders.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Delaying synthetic missing-result insertion could allow a temporarily invalid raw transcript shape until repair runs.

How is that risk mitigated?

The existing non-tool-result flush path still synthesizes genuinely missing results, and transcript repair now deterministically reorders late real results or synthesizes missing siblings before replay.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Await ClawSweeper and CI review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and maintainer review. No known author-side blocker.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Initial PR submission; no bot or reviewer comments yet.
